description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, bulging body constructed from vertical wooden staves. The staves are tightly bound by several dark metal hoops that encircle the barrel at the top, middle, and bottom, providing structural reinforcement. The wood has a rich, medium-brown color with visible grain patterns and some subtle weathering, suggesting it has been used or stored outdoors. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made from the same wood as the sides, with the top appearing to be securely closed and flush with the rim. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the surface finish is matte with a slightly rough texture. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or near taverns and markets.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with metal hoops, closed top, and a slightly weathered, matte finish. The surface shows wood grain and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full bulging profile, wood grain, and metal hoops; top is fully visible and closed.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view, similar to view 0, with the same features and no additional markings or handles visible.
description_view_2: Another rotated side view, showing the same wood and metal construction; no new features apparent.
description_view_3: Final side view, nearly identical to previous views, confirming the absence of handles or labels.
